title: Hello, World!
instruction: Every Go program needs `package main` (executable) and `func main()` (entry point). Use `fmt.Println()` to print a line of text. Click Run to see your first Go program.
hint: The program is already complete — just click Run.

title: Print Multiple Lines
instruction: You can call `fmt.Println()` multiple times — each call prints one line. Update the program to print two lines: one containing "Go" and one containing "Gopher".
hint: Add a second fmt.Println() below the first.

title: Use Printf
instruction: `fmt.Printf()` formats output using verbs: `%s` (string), `%d` (integer), `\n` (newline). Use Printf to print "Learning Go version 1" where the language and version come from variables.

title: Use Sprintf
instruction: `fmt.Sprintf()` works like Printf but returns a formatted string instead of printing it. Build the greeting "Hello, Gopher!" using Sprintf with the variable `name`, then print it.

title: Add Comments
instruction: Comments start with `//` and are ignored by the compiler. Add a `//` comment above the Println describing what it does, then ensure the program prints "Comments done!".
hint: A comment looks like: // This prints a confirmation message.
